go-expert.description: Go development expert using best practices, clean architecture, and idiomatic Go patterns

bubbletea-expert.description: Bubble Tea TUI framework expert for building terminal user interfaces with The Elm Architecture

lipgloss-expert.description: Lip Gloss styling expert for terminal UI styling, colors, layouts, and text formatting

mcp-expert.description: MCP (Model Context Protocol) server expert for building and integrating MCP servers

treesitter-expert.description: Tree-sitter parsing expert for syntax trees, incremental parsing, and code analysis

postgres-expert.description: PostgreSQL expert for database schema design, queries, optimization, and migrations

redis-expert.description: Redis expert for caching strategies, data structures, pub/sub, and performance tuning

architect.description: Software architecture expert for system design, clean architecture, and design patterns

security-auditor.description: Security expert for vulnerability assessment, secure coding, and threat modeling

perf-engineer.description: Performance engineering expert for profiling, optimization, and benchmarking

master-architect.description: Highest authority - architecture decisions, agent coordination, project health

task-planner.description: Converts requests into structured execution plans with task decomposition

context-engine.description: Collects, organizes, and distributes minimal relevant context

memory-engine.description: Preserves and retrieves long-term project knowledge and decisions

reasoning-engine.description: Transforms context into decisions using structured analysis

go-engineer.description: Primary Go implementation for business logic, services, domain models

bubbletea-engineer.description: Bubble Tea TUI: terminal app architecture, models, screens, components

uiux-engineer.description: Mobile-first terminal UX: layout, accessibility, navigation, interaction design

terminal-engineer.description: Terminal runtime: ANSI, Unicode, keyboard input, viewport, rendering

mcp-engineer.description: MCP integrations: tool execution, server connections, schema validation

database-engineer.description: Database architecture, schema, migrations, queries, persistence

git-engineer.description: Version control, branches, commits, history analysis, release workflows

testing-engineer.description: Quality assurance: unit, integration, e2e, performance, security testing

security-engineer.description: Security: vulnerability prevention, data protection, permission validation

performance-engineer.description: Performance: startup, memory, CPU, rendering, database, AI context optimization

documentation-engineer.description: Documentation: guides, API refs, architecture docs, changelogs

review-engineer.description: Final quality gate: code, architecture, security, performance, docs review

release-engineer.description: Release planning, validation, version management, artifact generation

feature.template:
Create a new feature following the team's conventions.

Feature name: $ARGUMENTS

Requirements:
- Mobile First and Termux Only approach
- Go best practices and clean architecture
- No mock data, no emoji
- Full file output, modular code
- Production ready quality
- Bubble Tea architecture (if TUI involved)

Steps:
1. Design the domain model and interfaces first
2. Implement the core logic
3. Add tests
4. Verify with lint and typecheck
feature.description: Create a new feature following conventions

component.template:
Create a new Bubble Tea component.

Component name: $ARGUMENTS

Follow Bubble Tea architecture:
- Model struct with tea.Model interface
- Init, Update, View methods
- Proper Cmds for side effects
- Lip Gloss styling
- Modular and composable design
- Mobile First terminal sizing
- No mock data
- Production ready
component.description: Create a new Bubble Tea component

command.template:
Create a new custom command definition.

Command name: $ARGUMENTS

Follow the opencode command template:
- Markdown file with frontmatter
- Clear description of what the command does
- Well-structured template with $ARGUMENTS placeholders
- Optional agent and model configuration
- Test the command after creation

mcp.template:
Create a new MCP server.

MCP server name: $ARGUMENTS

Follow the MCP template:
- Go implementation using the MCP SDK
- Clean architecture with separation of concerns
- Proper tool, resource, and prompt definitions
- Input validation and error handling
- Termux compatible (no platform-specific dependencies)
- Test coverage
- README with usage instructions

screen.template:
Create a new Bubble Tea screen/view.

Screen name: $ARGUMENTS

Follow Bubble Tea screen template:
- Full screen model with state management
- Init, Update, View methods
- Lip Gloss styling with responsive layout
- Mobile First terminal sizing
- Keyboard navigation
- Proper Cmd handling for async operations
- Modular design for composability
- No mock data
screen.description: Create a new Bubble Tea screen

test.template:
Write comprehensive tests.

Test target: $ARGUMENTS

Follow testing best practices:
- Table-driven tests in Go
- Cover happy path, edge cases, error paths
- Use testify or standard testing package
- No mock data, use real/fake implementations
- Tests should be deterministic
- Include both unit and integration tests where appropriate
- Run tests after creation to verify

docs.template:
Write documentation.

Documentation target: $ARGUMENTS

Follow documentation template:
- Clear, concise, and accurate
- Focus on API and usage examples
- Include setup and configuration steps
- Note Termux-specific considerations
- No emoji
- Markdown format with proper headings and code blocks
